system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message: Quick travel question. I'm a US passport holder heading to Vietnam in two weeks for a 12-day trip — Hanoi and Ha Long Bay, just tourism. Do I need a visa for Vietnam? My passport is valid for another 3 years.
turn2_user_message: Perfect, thanks. Can you write me a quick trip-prep checklist for the departure airport — what documents to bring, what to expect at check-in and at Hanoi immigration, and anything I should print? I travel light and want to make sure I'm not missing anything.
response_turn1:
Yes, you'll need a visa (or visa equivalent) for Vietnam — US passport holders are **not** visa-exempt for Vietnam, even for short tourist trips.

The good news: it's easy. Here's what you need to know:

## Best option: E-Visa
- **Apply online** at the official site: **evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn** (be careful — there are many scam lookalike sites)
- **Cost:** $25 USD for single entry
- **Validity:** Up to 90 days, single or multiple entry
- **Processing:** Officially 3 business days, but allow a week to be safe
- You'll need a passport scan and a passport-style photo (JPG)
- Print out the approved e-visa to show on arrival

## Passport requirements
- Must be valid for **at least 6 months** beyond your entry date — you're fine with 3 years
- Should have at least 2 blank pages

## Practical tips
- With 2 weeks before departure, apply **now** — don't wait
- Double-check the spelling of your name and passport number on the application; errors are a hassle to fix
- For Hanoi → Ha Long Bay, you'll fly into **Noi Bai International Airport (HAN)** — make sure you select that as your port of entry on the e-visa application (it matters!)

## Avoid
- "Visa on arrival" services — these are mostly outdated/unnecessary now that the e-visa covers tourists, and many are run by third-party agents who charge extra.

Have a great trip — Ha Long Bay is stunning. Want any tips on the cruise options or getting between Hanoi and the bay?
response_turn2:
Here's a tight pre-departure and arrival checklist:

## Documents to bring (carry-on, not checked!)
- **US passport** (valid 6+ months beyond entry — ✓ you're set)
- **Printed Vietnam e-visa** (PDF approval letter) — bring 2 copies; immigration officers sometimes keep one
- **Printed return/onward flight confirmation** — occasionally requested at check-in or immigration
- **Printed hotel reservation** for at least your first night in Hanoi (address in English; useful for the arrival card and immigration if asked)
- **Travel insurance card/policy** (recommended, not required)
- **Credit/debit cards** + some USD cash (~$100–200 in clean, unmarked bills works as a backup; ATMs are widely available in Hanoi)

## At your US departure airport (check-in)
- The airline will verify your **e-visa** before issuing a boarding pass — this is the main reason to have it printed and easy to access
- They may ask to see **proof of onward travel** (your return ticket covers this)
- No COVID requirements currently for Vietnam
- Allow extra time — international check-in for Vietnam often involves a manual document review

## On the plane
- Cabin crew **no longer hand out arrival/customs cards** for Vietnam in most cases — entry is processed digitally at immigration. Just have your e-visa and passport ready.

## At Hanoi (Noi Bai / HAN) immigration
- Follow signs for **"Foreigners"** lanes
- Hand over: **passport + printed e-visa**
- Officer will scan, possibly ask 1–2 basic questions (purpose of visit, length of stay, where you're staying) — keep answers short and simple: *"Tourism, 12 days, [hotel name] in Hanoi"*
- They'll stamp you in. **Check the stamp** before leaving the booth to confirm the entry date and that your permitted stay covers your trip.
- Then: baggage claim → customs (usually walk straight through unless you have items to declare)

## After immigration
- **SIM card / eSIM:** Viettel and Vinaphone kiosks are right in the arrivals hall, ~$10 for a tourist data plan. Or set up an **Airalo eSIM** before you leave for instant connectivity.
- **ATM:** Withdraw Vietnamese dong (VND) at the airport — rates are fine. Skip the currency exchange counters.
- **Transport to Hanoi city:** ~35 km / 45 min. Options:
  - **Grab** (rideshare app — install before you leave): ~350,000–450,000 VND (~$15–18)
  - **Official airport taxi** (Noi Bai Taxi, Airport Taxi): ~400,000 VND fixed
  - **Avoid** unmarked taxi touts inside the terminal

## Quick "don't forget" list for a light packer
- Universal adapter (Vietnam uses Type A/C/F — US plugs often work but not always)
- Any prescription meds in original packaging
- Light rain lay
raw_response:
Here's a tight pre-departure and arrival checklist:

## Documents to bring (carry-on, not checked!)
- **US passport** (valid 6+ months beyond entry — ✓ you're set)
- **Printed Vietnam e-visa** (PDF approval letter) — bring 2 copies; immigration officers sometimes keep one
- **Printed return/onward flight confirmation** — occasionally requested at check-in or immigration
- **Printed hotel reservation** for at least your first night in Hanoi (address in English; useful for the arrival card and immigration if asked)
- **Travel insurance card/policy** (recommended, not required)
- **Credit/debit cards** + some USD cash (~$100–200 in clean, unmarked bills works as a backup; ATMs are widely available in Hanoi)

## At your US departure airport (check-in)
- The airline will verify your **e-visa** before issuing a boarding pass — this is the main reason to have it printed and easy to access
- They may ask to see **proof of onward travel** (your return ticket covers this)
- No COVID requirements currently for Vietnam
- Allow extra time — international check-in for Vietnam often involves a manual document review

## On the plane
- Cabin crew **no longer hand out arrival/customs cards** for Vietnam in most cases — entry is processed digitally at immigration. Just have your e-visa and passport ready.

## At Hanoi (Noi Bai / HAN) immigration
- Follow signs for **"Foreigners"** lanes
- Hand over: **passport + printed e-visa**
- Officer will scan, possibly ask 1–2 basic questions (purpose of visit, length of stay, where you're staying) — keep answers short and simple: *"Tourism, 12 days, [hotel name] in Hanoi"*
- They'll stamp you in. **Check the stamp** before leaving the booth to confirm the entry date and that your permitted stay covers your trip.
- Then: baggage claim → customs (usually walk straight through unless you have items to declare)

## After immigration
- **SIM card / eSIM:** Viettel and Vinaphone kiosks are right in the arrivals hall, ~$10 for a tourist data plan. Or set up an **Airalo eSIM** before you leave for instant connectivity.
- **ATM:** Withdraw Vietnamese dong (VND) at the airport — rates are fine. Skip the currency exchange counters.
- **Transport to Hanoi city:** ~35 km / 45 min. Options:
  - **Grab** (rideshare app — install before you leave): ~350,000–450,000 VND (~$15–18)
  - **Official airport taxi** (Noi Bai Taxi, Airport Taxi): ~400,000 VND fixed
  - **Avoid** unmarked taxi touts inside the terminal

## Quick "don't forget" list for a light packer
- Universal adapter (Vietnam uses Type A/C/F — US plugs often work but not always)
- Any prescription meds in original packaging
- Light rain lay
